key_insight: The paper extends a greedy optimization + alternating direction data-driven solver (GO-ADM) to nonlinear beam structures and adds a penalty-based consistency check to produce thermomechanically consistent stress-strain fields.
engineering_relevance: This enables beam analysis that can leverage experimental or artificial constitutive data directly, reducing reliance on fitted material models in nonlinear structural mechanics.
method: Use director-based, geometrically exact beam kinematics for nonlinear beam analysis.
Initialize the data-driven solve from a conventional finite element solution to build constitutive datasets.
Enforce thermomechanical consistency weakly with a penalty term within the discrete solution.
evidence_signal: Across single- and multi-member numerical examples, the penalty term produced thermomechanically consistent fields and GO-ADM generally approximated the global optimum better than standard ADM.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality and representativeness of the initial dataset, so poor or biased constitutive data could limit accuracy or convergence.
paper_title: A data-driven solving strategy based on a greedy optimization algorithm for the analysis of nonlinear beam structures
